title: PV1 Camera Configuration
description:
PV1 Camera Configuration

PV1 Camera Configuration

(1)    Start the Epson RC+ 8.0.

(2)    Select [System Configuration] from the Setup menu.

(3)    Click [Vision]-[Cameras] from the tree.

(4)     Click the [Add] button to add a camera.

To search the cameras on the network, select "Search for a camera" and click [OK].

The following dialog box appears if the camera is found on the network.  Select the camera to add and click the [Add] button.

If you have information of the camera required for setting such as IP address, select "Manually configure a camera".

(5)     Select the camera model and ID as necessary.

For the GigE camera, select the ID after selecting the camera model.  The ID is a serial number of the camera.

Selecting the ID displays the IP address set to the camera.

(6)  If you changed settings after adding the camera, click the [Apply] button.

(7)  Repeat steps (4) through (6) to add cameras.

TIP:

Up to 16 cameras (8 CV2 cameras and 8 PV1 cameras) can be configured.

When starting more than one Epson RC+ 8.0 by one PC, PV1 camera is only available in the first session.

Item	Description

Name	This is the name of the camera.  The
		 maximum length is 16.

IP Address	This is the IP address of the GigE
		 camera.

Refer to section Setup 2. Software Configuration
		 for details.

Type	Sets the type of camera.

Model	Sets the model of camera.

Add	Adds a camera.  It is also
		 possible to search and add the cameras on the network.

Delete	Delete the currently selected camera.

Apply	Set current values after changes have
		 been made.

For CV2: When this button is clicked,
		 RC+ attempts to connect with the camera to verify the IP address
		 and determine the camera type.

Restore	Revert back to the previous values.

Configure...	Click this button to open the [Camera
		 configuration] dialog box.

Reset	For CV2 only.

Resets the currently selected CV2.  All
		 project files are cleared and the CV2 is restarted.  Restart
		 takes approximately one minute.

Update Firmware...	For CV2 only.

Updates the CV2 firmware.  For
		 more details, refer to the following section.

Hardware  1.3.10 Updating CV2 Firmware
